items.title: Claude Platform Release Notes
items.author: docs.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has been active with major announcements in early 2026. In February, they released two flagship models: Claude Opus 4.6 (Feb 5), positioned as an industry-leading model for agentic coding, computer use, tool use, and finance; and Claude Sonnet 4.6 (Feb 17), delivering frontier performance across coding, agents, and professional work at scale. Anthropic also clarified that Claude will remain ad-free, citing advertising incentives as incompatible with a genuinely helpful AI assistant. A

items.title: Claude Platform Release Notes - Anthropic API Docs
items.author: platform.claude.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has had a strong start to 2026 with several major model releases. Claude Opus 4.6 launched on February 5, featuring a 1,000,000-token context window, improved multi-step reasoning, stronger coding and financial analysis capabilities, and a 14.5-hour task completion time horizon — the longest of any AI model as of early 2026. Claude Sonnet 4.6 followed on February 17, bringing a full upgrade across coding, computer use, long-context reasoning, agent planning, and design, also with a 1M

items.title: Claude Developer Platform - Claude API Docs
items.author: docs.anthropic.com
items.snippet:
Anthropic has released several significant API updates in early 2026. Claude Sonnet 4.6 launched as the latest balanced model with improved agentic search performance, fewer token consumption, extended thinking support, and a 1M token context window (beta). Claude Haiku 3 (claude-3-haiku-20240307) is being deprecated with retirement scheduled for April 19, 2026, with migration recommended to Claude Haiku 4.5. The 1M token context window is now available in beta for Claude Opus 4.6 as well.

On t

items.title: Reasoning Models Don't Always Say What They Think
items.author: assets.anthropic.com
items.snippet: Anthropic has been actively publishing research across multiple domains in early 2026. On the alignment front, notable papers include 'Natural Emergent Misalignment from Reward Hacking in Production RL' and 'Reasoning Models Don't Always Say What They Think,' both examining failure modes in frontier AI systems. The 'Hot Mess of AI' post on the Alignment Science Blog (February 2026) decomposes misalignment errors into bias and variance components as model intelligence and task complexity scale. A

items.title: Anthropic Release Notes - March 2026 | Releasebot
items.author: releasebot.io
items.snippet:
Anthropic has had a highly active March 2026. The most significant recent development is a legal victory on March 26, 2026: a federal judge granted Anthropic a preliminary injunction against the Trump administration's Pentagon ban, with the court citing 'First Amendment retaliation.' CEO Dario Amodei had publicly stated he would not allow Claude to be used for autonomous weapons or to surveil American citizens, which reportedly triggered the DOD's 'supply chain risk' designation.

On the product
